title: sorbus umbellata subsp. koevessii
intro: Sorbus umbellata subsp. koevessii, a lesser-known subspecies of the American mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthelmintic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Sorbus umbellata subsp. koevessii include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical treatments.
